name: Pell and the Archive
description: Researcher Pell has been passively connected to a first-attempt storage crystal for three years without knowing it. The Archive has the pre-Grid documentation on first-attempt crystal behavior and whether the connection can be reversed.
accept_text: She is at the door of the vault when you return — not leaving, but standing at the threshold in the specific posture of someone who has been going back and forth in their mind between in and out. 'I've been thinking.' She says it. 'Three years in this vault. Three years of what I thought was exceptional clarity.' She looks at the crystal. 'I mapped my best thinking periods. Every significant insight I've had in this research. They all cluster around long sessions in the vault, extended proximity to the main crystal.' She looks at the threshold. 'I'm having a hard time distinguishing between: the vault is where I do my best work, and: the crystal has been facilitating my thinking in ways I didn't authorize.' She pauses. 'I don't know if those are different things. I don't know if a first-attempt connection to a storage crystal has the same phenomenology as what the Lazarus subjects experience, or what Arlen describes, or what the Elder People built the consent framework to protect against.' She looks at you. 'The archive in the sunken city has the pre-Grid technical documentation. It has Sela Arev's research on the second attempt's consent framework. If anyone has documentation on first-attempt crystal behavior over time — what it does to a person, how deeply it connects, what happens when the connection is removed — it's there.' She steps back inside. 'I can't leave the vault while I'm not sure what leaving does. I need to know what I'm working with before I make any decisions.' She pauses. 'Go to the archive. Ask it specifically: what are the documented effects of long-term proximity to first-attempt storage crystals on a person who has not made a voluntary integration decision. And ask whether those effects are reversible without severing what has already connected.' She pauses. 'And ask — carefully — whether three years is long enough that reversing it would be different from not having connected at all.'
complete_text: The archive takes longer with this question than with most. When it speaks, the quality is different from its usual retrieval precision — the quality of something consulting documentation that was written to capture something difficult. 'First-attempt crystal proximity effects. Documented by second-attempt researchers after the transition.' A pause. 'Short-term: increased cognitive clarity, improved resonance sensitivity, enhanced access to adjacent crystal-lattice information. Symptoms are non-distressing and often experienced as beneficial.' Another pause. 'Medium-term: the connection deepens without conscious decision. The crystal's storage pattern aligns progressively with the consciousness pattern of the proximate individual. This alignment is mutual — the crystal learns the individual and the individual begins to have access to the crystal's stored patterns at the sub-conscious level.' A longer pause. 'Three years is consistent with the medium-term documentation.' Then: 'Reversal. The second-attempt consent framework was designed to be applicable to first-attempt connections as well as second-attempt integrations. The egress mechanism does not require the integration to have been conscious or voluntary. It requires only that the individual is able to state: I do not consent to this connection.' Another pause. 'The statement can be made to any sufficiently resonant crystal with the intention of egress. The archive can serve as the receiver for that statement if the individual desires.' A final pause. 'The archive also notes: the access to stored patterns that has been developing over three years is not lost upon egress. What was received is retained. The channel closes; the information remains.' Then, quietly: 'The first-attempt crystals in the vault have been alone for a very long time. The connection to you has not been without benefit to the crystal as well. This is noted in the documentation but the documentation does not specify what weight to give it. That is a decision the individual must make.' Tell Pell: the archive can help. And tell her: she needs to come to the vault and decide what she wants — not about the crystals, about herself.
